evals.prompt: Generate C4 architecture diagrams for the rfpilot product from the graphify graph at graphify-out/products/rfpilot/graph.json - a container diagram plus a component diagram per service.
evals.expected_output: context.puml + container.puml + one component .puml per owned container + index.md manifest; validate.py reports PASS (zero syntax errors, full coverage).

assertions.text: validate.py exits 0 (VALIDATION PASSED) on the output dir
one component diagram exists per owned container (manifest denominator == covered + logged skips)
no diagram contains an invalid macro (no Rel_Bi / unknown C4 macro)

evals.prompt: I don't use graphify. Scan this repo and draw me a C4 diagram of the services and how they talk to each other, in mermaid so it shows on GitHub.
evals.expected_output: repo-scan skeleton enriched into an IR, built as mermaid (.mmd) context+container+components, index.md manifest, validation clean.

assertions.text: .mmd files are produced for context, container, and each owned container
containers detected correspond to real deployable units (manifest dirs)

evals.prompt: Here is an architecture.yaml for a planned payments system. Turn it into C4 PlantUML and Mermaid, and tell me if any container is missing a component breakdown.
evals.expected_output: both formats rendered from the yaml-derived IR; manifest flags any owned container as 'skipped: no components defined'; validation clean.

assertions.text: both .puml and .mmd are generated for every level
manifest explicitly lists each owned container with generated/skipped status
